page.content: In the analysis of extracts, AMS-I yielded 3.74 ± 0.13 Polyphenols, 0.10 ± 0.02 Flavonoids, 10.78 ± 0.16 Antioxidants, and 0.54 ± 0.05 Ferric reducing antioxidant power; AMS-II produced 2.35 ± 0.08 Polyphenols, 0.13 ± 0.08 Flavonoids, 8.89 ± 0.04 Antioxidants, and 0.46 ± 0.03 Ferric reducing antioxidant power; AMS-III showed 1.73 ± 0.02 Polyphenols, 0.22 ± 0.03 Flavonoids, 9.12 ± 0.06 Antioxidants, and 0.38 ± 0.04 Ferric reducing antioxidant power; AMS-IV had 1.56 ± 0.02 Polyphenols, 0.26 ± 0.02 Flavonoids, 8.08 ± 0.08 Antioxidants, and 0.42 ± 0.04 Ferric reducing antioxidant power; AML-I displayed 9.49 ± 0.17 Polyphenols, 5.60 ± 0.02 Flavonoids, 13.30 ± 0.33 Antioxidants, and 3.02 ± 0.05 Ferric reducing antioxidant power; AML-II revealed 11.86 ± 0.11 Polyphenols, 2.00 ± 0.08 Flavonoids, 15.30 ± 0.15 Antioxidants, and 4.51 ± 0.04 Ferric reducing antioxidant power; AML-III presented 13.38 ± 0.26 Polyphenols, 3.12 ± 0.03 Flavonoids, 19.85 ± 0.07 Antioxidants, and 6.07 ± 0.02 Ferric reducing antioxidant power; AML-IV recorded 13.84 ± 0.10 Polyphenols, 6.00 ± 0.02 Flavonoids, 21.52 ± 0.07 Antioxidants, and 6.28 ± 0.01 Ferric reducing antioxidant power.

page.content: In the study of Drotrecogin alfa (activated) versus placebo, among all randomly assigned patients, there were 1,316 in the treatment group with 243 (18.47) died, and 1,297 in the placebo group with 221 (17.04) died, resulting in a relative risk of 1.08 with a 95% CI of 0.92, 1.28 and a Breslow-Day P value of —. For the first patient only, the treatment group had 260 with 58 (22.31) died, and the placebo group had 249 with 36 (14.46) died, yielding a relative risk of 1.54 with a 95% CI of 1.06, 2.25 and a Breslow-Day P value of —.

page.content: Excluding the first patient, the treatment group comprised 1,056 with 185 (17.52) died, and the placebo group had 1,048 with 185 (17.65) died, resulting in a relative risk of 0.99 with a 95% CI of 0.82, 1.19 and a Breslow-Day P value of 0.04.
